count.description: The number of items in the data returned.

per_page.description: The amount to be loaded per page. This matches the 'limit' parameter that the endpoint was called with.

PaginationIds.title: First and last IDs
PaginationIds.description: Array of first and last ID in the items, to easily use for getting next or previous items.

first.description: The ID of the first item in the returned list of items.

last.description: The ID of the last item in the returned list of items.

PaginationLinks.title: Pagination links
PaginationLinks.description: Pagination links for easy navigation to next and previous items.

next.description: The URL to call in order to get the next page of items.

prev.description: The URL to call in order to get the prev page of items.

self.description: The URL to call in order to get the current page of items.

created_by.name: created_by
created_by.description: Retrieve only the items created by this user

after_id.name: after_id
after_id.description: A cursor for use in pagination. after_id is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with ID 150, your subsequent call can include after_id=150 in order to fetch the next page of the list.

before_id.name: before_id
before_id.description: A cursor for use in pagination. before_id is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with 250, your subsequent call can include before_id=250 in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned, between 1 and 100.

order.name: order
order.description: Direction you want the data to load. Use 'asc' for oldest -> newest, and 'desc' for newest -> oldest.

start_date.name: start_date
start_date.description: Start date filter for data returned. Checks for items created or updated after this date.

end_date.name: end_date
end_date.description: End date filter for data returned. Checks for items created or updated before this date.

tags.name: Introduction
tags.description:
The Ecanvasser API is based on RESTful principles; we also use HTTP authentication standards and HTTP methods to make the Ecanvasser API as easy to use as possible.

The Ecanvasser API doesn't support bulk updates. You can work on only one object per request.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
API keys are sensitive credentials that should be kept secret and secure. Sharing them in publicly accessible areas like GitHub or client-side code can leave your account vulnerable to unauthorized access or malicious attacks.

The Ecanvasser API uses API keys to authenticate requests. All API requests must be made over HTTPS. Calls made over plain HTTP will fail.

API requests without authentication will also fail.

You can manage your API key in the Ecanvasser Dashboard.

Each API key is specific to an Ecanvasser account, not an individual user.

Before you get started, you will need to contact support@ecanvasser.com to enable the Public API feature within your Ecanvasser account.

When the API feature has been enabled, login to your Ecanvasser account and navigate to the Account Settings. From there, select Public API and follow the steps to generate your unique API key.

Click on COPY ACCESS TOKEN which will copy the access token to your clipboard, which you can forward to your developers to use in your chosen application.

For security purposes, we will not display the API key again once you have generated the key, therefore you must copy the key at this stage otherwise, you will need to revoke the key and generate a new key.

Related guide: Access your API Key

tags.name: Postman
tags.description:
Once you have obtained an access token, you can try out our API using Postman.

Click the Run in Postman button. This will allow you to clone the Postman collection and start sending API requests.

After you have cloned the collection, you just need to Variables tab on the homescreen of the collection, and paste your access token into the Current value field for the bearerToken variable.

If you are using the browser version of Postman, you may need to change baseUrl value. Use one of these values, depending on your geographic location.

If you are located in a North American or South American country, use:
https://public-api-usa.ecanvasser.com

For all other countries, use:
https://public-api-eu.ecanvasser.com

Then click Save, and you should be all set to start playing around with the Ecanvasser API

tags.name: Errors
tags.description:
Ecanvasser uses conventional HTTP response codes to indicate the success or failure of an API request. In general:

Codes in the 2xx range indicate success.

Codes in the 4xx range indicate an error that failed given the information provided for example, a required parameter was omitted

Codes in the 5xx range indicate an error with Ecanvasser's servers (these are rare).

tags.name: Pagination
tags.description:
All top-level API resources have support for bulk fetches via "list" API methods. For instance, you can contacts, houses, interactions etc. These list API methods share a common structure, taking at least these four parameters: limit, order, after_id, and before_id.

The response of a list API method represents a single page in a reverse chronological stream of objects. If you do not specify after_id or before_id, you will receive the first page of this stream.

The size of the page is determined by the number you pass in the limit query parameter. This defaults to 100, but can be set anywhere between 1 - 1000.

Order can be ascending or descending, ascending for oldest first, descending for newest first.

In order to get more data, you can use after_id (with ID set to the last ID in your current list of items) to get the next page of items, or before_id to get (with ID set to the first ID in your current list of items). Only one of after_id or before_id may be used.

tags.name: Rate Limit
tags.description:
The Ecanvasser API uses a number of safeguards against bursts of incoming traffic to help maximize its stability. Users who send many requests in quick succession might see error responses that show up as status code 429.

Our rate limit is 300 requests per minute. If you exceed this, any subsequent requests will get a 429 response.

tags.name: Appointment
tags.description:
In Ecanvasser, users have the ability to schedule, manage and track Appointments with contacts or households directly within the platform.

Users can schedule an appointment with a contact or at an address, specifying the date, time, location and additional notes. This is beneficial for teams conducting outreach, sales, or engagement activities, making it easy to manage schedules as part of a broader sales or engagement funnel.

Related Guide: Understanding the appointment scheduler

tags.name: Contact
tags.description:
In the context of customer relationship management (CRM) or political campaigning, a Contact refers to a person whose personal information such as name, email address, phone number, or physical address which has either been collected from the person or imported from a contact file. Organizations may use Contacts for various purposes such as sending marketing emails, making field sales door-knocking campaigns, or canvassing for political campaigns.

Contacts can be segmented and organized based on various criteria such as demographic information, interests, location, or engagement history. This allows organizations to target specific groups of contacts with personalized messaging and increase the effectiveness of their outreach efforts.

Related Guide: Understanding the contact database

tags.name: Custom field
tags.description:
A custom field is a field that can be added to a Contact's personal profile or object to capture specific information that is not already included in the standard fields provided by the Ecanvasser software.

Custom fields can be used to capture information that is unique to a particular organization's needs or goals. For example, a political campaign might create custom fields to track a contact's voting history, party affiliation, or likelihood of supporting the campaign. Custom fields can also be used to capture more detailed or granular information about a contact, such as their job title, industry, or interests. By adding custom fields to a Contact's profile, organizations can better understand and segment their contacts, and tailor their messaging and outreach efforts accordingly.

Related guide: Capturing data with Custom Fields

tags.name: Document
tags.description:
In Ecanvasser, a document refers to a digital file that can be uploaded and stored within the software's platform. Documents can include a variety of file types, such as

PDFs, images, spreadsheets, and word-processing documents. They can be used to store and share important information related to a campaign, such as policy briefings, campaign materials, or supporter lists.

Ecanvasser also provides a document management system that allows users to organize and categorize their documents, making it easy to find and access the information they need quickly and efficiently. Overall, the document management features in Ecanvasser help streamline the campaign workflow and ensure that all team members have access to the information they need to be successful.

Related Guide: Document Repository

tags.name: Follow-up request
tags.description:
A follow-up request refers to a request from a Contact (such as a lead, voter or supporter) by a User (such as an organizer or volunteer) after initial contact has been made with them.

When a User interacts with a Contact, the User can record the outcome of the interaction in the Ecanvasser software. This could be a pledge to vote, a commitment to volunteer, or any other relevant information. As part of the interaction record, the User may log a follow-up request for a member of the team to follow up with the contact.

Related Guide: Understanding follow-up requests

tags.name: House
tags.description:
A House typically refers to a physical property or residence that is identified and marked on a map within the software's database. Houses can be used as a starting point for canvassing efforts, as organizers can visit the House to gather information or build support for a campaign, organization, or cause.

Each House object typically contains information such as the address (including number/unit, street name, city, state) and geo coordinates.

A House can have 0, 1 or many occupants which are known as Contacts.

tags.name: Interaction
tags.description:
An Interaction refers to any type of engagement or contact that occurs between a User and a Contact. This can include a wide range of activities, such as door-to-door canvassing, phone calls, emails, or other forms of outreach.

Each interaction is recorded in the software's database, along with relevant information such as the date, time, location, and outcome of the interaction including the rating of the interaction. Users can also add notes or comments to each interaction, providing additional context or details about the engagement.

Related guide: Understanding interactions

tags.name: Survey
tags.description:
A Survey refers to a set of questions that are designed to gather information from voters or constituents on a specific issue or topic. Surveys can be created and customized within the software and can be used to collect a wide range of data. Surveys can be assigned to specific Users or Teams within the software and can be completed by Users in the field using the Ecanvasser mobile app.

Related guide: Creating and managing surveys

tags.name: Team
tags.description:
A Team refers to a group of Users. Typically the team are working together towards a common goal, such as a specific campaign or sales effort. A User can be part of one or many Teams. Teams can be assigned dedicated lists of contacts to canvass or interact with. They can also be assigned dedicated Talking Points and Surveys

Related guide: Members Directory and Managing Teams

tags.name: User
tags.description:
A User refers to an individual who has been granted access to Ecanvassers’ software features and tools by an administrator or account owner.

Users can include field sales reps, sales managers, campaign staff, volunteers, or other individuals who are involved in canvassing, outreach, or field sales outreach campaigns.Users can be assigned to teams and have lists of Contacts assigned to them as part of the field operations. This helps to ensure that outreach efforts are well-coordinated and that each User knows which contacts they are responsible for engaging with.

Related guide: Onboarding your team
